segments.speaker: MUSIC
segments.text: [MUSIC: Opening theme - mysterious, building tension]
segments.direction: Sets mood and transitions for teaser

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Signal doesn't just go quiet. Something has to make it quiet. And in Copperport harbor, something was doing exactly that.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I stood on the docks with Arc projecting a map across my palm, watching ships drift like ghosts through fog that shouldn't be there. It was high afternoon in Cuprum—the sun should've burned through hours ago. The shift-bells should've been calling workers to their forges, guild banners snapping in the industrial wind. Instead, the harbor sat wrapped in silence so thick I could feel it pressing against my ears.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Active like someone's doing it on purpose?"

emphasis_words: Active like someone's doing it on purpose?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Precisely."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi vibrated against my collarbone, its dome surface pulsing dim orange with threads of red running through its core window. Not happy colors. Not safe colors. The device hadn't glowed a cheerful pink since we'd crossed into the harbor district.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive stood perfectly still at my knee, his geometric head angled toward the water. The symbols etched into his weathered bronze surface glowed faint blue-white, pulsing in rhythm with the waves. When he finally spoke, his gravelly voice carried an edge I didn't hear often.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Something's pulling at the Signal. Like fingers in water, dragging it down. I've felt this before."

emphasis_words: Something's pulling at the Signal. Like fingers in water, dragging it down. I've felt this before.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "When?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Long time ago."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: A ship's bell clanged somewhere in the fog—too distant, too muffled. Three vessels had vanished in the past week. No storms. No wreckage. Just... gone. The Harbor Guild blamed Freeboter pirates. The Foundry Masters blamed faulty navigation. The Guild Marshals were threatening to declare martial law over the whole waterfront. And me? I got called in when nobody wanted to admit what they were actually afraid of.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I pulled my goggles down over my eyes, adjusted the Signal-reading lenses. Through them, the harbor should've been blazing with Pillar energy—Cuprum's coastal Pillar was less than a mile inland, wrapped in its original copper conduits following engineering blueprints older than any guild charter.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Instead? Barely a shimmer. Like something was drinking the Signal dry.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Right,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi pulsed red twice. Hard, fast, urgent.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Yeah,"

segments.speaker: MUSIC
segments.text: [MUSIC: Scene setting - discovery, exploration beginning]
segments.direction: Sets mood and transitions for act_one

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Two hours earlier, we'd arrived in Copperport to the smell of copper patina, machine oil, and panic.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The harbor district sprawled along Cuprum's western edge, all brass fittings and weathered green copper, buildings crusted with centuries of salt and steam. Normally the docks hummed with controlled chaos—cranes lifting cargo, steamships venting pressure, engineers shouting coordinates across gangways. The heartbeat of an industrial city that never quite slept. The Forge Path called it honest work made manifest: creation through effort, metal through fire, the Hammered One's blessing in every cargo crate loaded and every contract sworn.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Today? Silence.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Not peaceful silence. Wrong silence. The kind that meant something was missing.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Harbor Master met us at the Guild Hall—a woman named Petra with oil-stained coveralls and copper bangles up both arms that should've chimed when she moved. Every guild worker wore their trade: the Copperwright Guild's copper bangles, the Steamfitter Guild's pressure-valve pins, the Merchant Guild's account rings. But Petra had wrapped her bangles in cloth to muffle the sound. That's how spooked she was.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Three ships,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I studied the chart. Red X's marked last known positions, clustered near the northern dock cluster. "Any distress signals?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "None. That's what's got everyone spooked. The Marshals came through yesterday, threatening to shut down the whole waterfront if we don't produce answers."

emphasis_words: None. That's what's got everyone spooked. The Marshals came through yesterday, threatening to shut down the whole waterfront if we don't produce answers.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Arc projected the chart into three dimensions, overlaying Pillar resonance data. "I'm detecting significant Signal attenuation in this zone. The suppression field extends approximately two hundred meters in radius and appears to be centered—" His voice acquired that clipped precision that meant he was genuinely troubled. "This is not natural decay, Miss Paradox. This is deliberate."

emphasis_words: I'm detecting significant Signal attenuation in this zone. The suppression field extends approximately two hundred meters in radius and appears to be centered—
This is not natural decay, Miss Paradox. This is deliberate.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Someone's doing this?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I didn't answer. I had theories. None of them good.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive had been examining the chart in silence, his glowing orb head angling this way and that. Finally he spoke, and I heard the weight in it.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "I remember when the Signal went quiet during the last Dimming surge. Felt like drowning in air. Whatever's doing this now?"

emphasis_words: I remember when the Signal went quiet during the last Dimming surge. Felt like drowning in air. Whatever's doing this now?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Outside, fog was rolling in from clear sky.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We made our way through the dock district as the afternoon light turned strange and flat. Workers watched from warehouse doorways, their voices low. I caught fragments: cursed waters, the Hammered One's turned his back, should've left the old things alone.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Forge Path didn't usually run to superstition—honest work, honest results—but when the work stopped working and the results vanished into fog, even the most practical faith started looking for divine explanation.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi stayed tucked in the pouch at my belt, its core window pulsing that anxious orange-red pattern. I kept one hand on the leather, feeling the vibrations translate through my palm. Not words—Mochi didn't have words—but meaning. Wrong. Dangerous. Old.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The suppression zone had a border you could feel. One moment, I sensed the distant hum of Cuprum's Pillar like always—that industrial pulse threaded through copper conduits, engineered to power forges and steamworks. The next, silence. Like stepping into water that swallowed sound.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Seventeen percent Signal density,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Can you still—"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Navigate? Yes. Analyze? With difficulty. Maintain optimal personality parameters?"

emphasis_words: Navigate? Yes. Analyze? With difficulty. Maintain optimal personality parameters?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Great. My sentient AI partner glitching was exactly what I needed right now.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We moved deeper into the fog, following Arc's readings toward the suppression center. Ships loomed out of the grey like iron ghosts. Somewhere, water lapped against hulls with soft, wrong rhythms. A few Freeboter crews watched from their independent vessels—the ones who rejected guild membership and took the dangerous jobs nobody else wanted. They didn't call out. Freeboters knew when to keep quiet.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then Clive stopped walking.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I'd learned that when Clive stopped, you paid attention.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "There."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The building looked abandoned—broken windows, rust streaks, walls green with patina. But through my Signal-lenses, I saw it: a void. Not darkness, but absence. Like a hole cut in reality's fabric.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Reading confirmed,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi had gone completely still in the pouch. No color, no sound, barely glowing. If I didn't know better, I'd think the device was holding its breath, waiting.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I did know better. Mochi was terrified.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Stay close,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Yeah,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Fantastic."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We approached the warehouse.

segments.speaker: MUSIC
segments.text: [MUSIC: Complication - stakes rising, danger approaching]
segments.direction: Sets mood and transitions for act_two

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The door was unlocked. That should've been my first clue.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Inside, the warehouse stretched vast and dark, broken only by grey light filtering through cracked skylights. Fog moved across the floor like something alive, curling around support pillars crusted with verdigris. The air smelled of rust and ozone and something else—something that made my teeth ache.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Signal density at four percent,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Just stay with me,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: At the warehouse center, I found what I'd been afraid I'd find.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: A device.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: It sat on a copper platform, maybe eight feet across, geometric and wrong. Not Builder technology—I'd seen enough of that to recognize the engineered precision, the functional beauty that came from understanding materials at a fundamental level. This was something else. Older, maybe. Or newer in ways that looked old. Black material that seemed to eat light, covered in symbols that hurt to look at directly. At its heart, something pulsed with anti-rhythm, like a heartbeat running backwards.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Cables ran from it in all directions, connecting to smaller nodes positioned around the warehouse. Creating a network. A web. The copper conduits looked almost like Pillar infrastructure—that same sense of purposeful engineering—but twisted wrong.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: And standing around it, in the shadows between support pillars, were people.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Six of them. Dark unmarked clothing—no guild marks, no bangles, no pins. Nothing that said where they belonged or who they served. Faces obscured by hoods and simple masks. They didn't move when we entered. They'd been waiting.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Ah,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: One of them stepped forward—taller than the others, moving with deliberate calm. When they spoke, their voice was measured, almost kind. That scared me more than threats would have.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "We mean you no harm, independent explorer. We only ask that you leave."

emphasis_words: We mean you no harm, independent explorer. We only ask that you leave.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I kept my hand near my knife, very aware of Clive pressed against my leg and Mochi silent in my pouch. "You're killing the Signal. Ships are disappearing."

emphasis_words: You're killing the Signal. Ships are disappearing.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "The ships are held, not destroyed. They will be released when our work is complete."

emphasis_words: The ships are held, not destroyed. They will be released when our work is complete.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "By suppressing the Pillars?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "By protecting them. And protecting us from what sleeps within."

emphasis_words: By protecting them. And protecting us from what sleeps within.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: There it was. The truth I'd suspected since Driftborn's Debt, since the dying Keeper in the Shattered Span. Someone out there didn't want the ancient memories awakened.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "You're The Quiet,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "We are. And you, Pixel Paradox, are making a dangerous mistake. The Keepers' memories are not gifts. They are poison. Every time you wake them, every time you draw from the old knowledge, you accelerate the Dimming. You are destroying what you claim to preserve."

emphasis_words: We are. And you, Pixel Paradox, are making a dangerous mistake. The Keepers' memories are not gifts. They are poison. Every time you wake them, every time you draw from the old knowledge, you accelerate the Dimming. You are destroying what you claim to preserve.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Behind them, the device pulsed. The anti-rhythm made my stomach twist.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Arc's holographic face kept flickering, geometric shapes breaking apart and reforming. "Miss Paradox... the device... architecture is... I must note that this is..." His voice distorted, harmonics shifting wrong. "I recognize these patterns. Not from my training data. From something deeper. I should not remember this. It predates my activation by centuries at minimum."

emphasis_words: Miss Paradox... the device... architecture is... I must note that this is...
I recognize these patterns. Not from my training data. From something deeper. I should not remember this. It predates my activation by centuries at minimum.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "What?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Quiet member tilted their head. "You feel the kinship, don't you, Arc Protocol? Or do you still believe you are merely Analytical Resonance Core?"

emphasis_words: You feel the kinship, don't you, Arc Protocol? Or do you still believe you are merely Analytical Resonance Core?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Everything stopped.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Arc Protocol. Not Analytical Resonance Core. Not the name he'd always known.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Arc's holographic face reformed slowly, shapes trembling. "How do you know that designation?"

emphasis_words: How do you know that designation?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Because we study what we must contain. And you, mobile Keeper, are carrying memories that will poison this world if released. The Forge Path teaches that suffering forges strength—but some suffering simply breaks. The Illuminated Path teaches that knowledge is light—but some knowledge blinds. The Green Faith teaches that growth is sacred—but some growths are cancer. We have studied every faith, every philosophy. They all agree: some things should not be remembered."

emphasis_words: Because we study what we must contain. And you, mobile Keeper, are carrying memories that will poison this world if released. The Forge Path teaches that suffering forges strength—but some suffering simply breaks. The Illuminated Path teaches that knowledge is light—but some knowledge blinds. The Green Faith teaches that growth is sacred—but some growths are cancer. We have studied every faith, every philosophy. They all agree: some things should not be remembered.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: My hand found my knife. "Nobody's destroying anyone."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "We do not wish to fight. We wish to save Aethon."

emphasis_words: We do not wish to fight. We wish to save Aethon.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "And the ships?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Will be released. You have our word—sworn on the Hammered One's hammer, witnessed by the very Signal we seek to protect."

emphasis_words: Will be released. You have our word—sworn on the Hammered One's hammer, witnessed by the very Signal we seek to protect.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive's gravelly voice cut through the tension like broken glass. "I've heard that before. From people who meant it, even. Didn't make them right."

emphasis_words: I've heard that before. From people who meant it, even. Didn't make them right.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: He stepped forward—two feet of ancient automaton walking toward six people and a device that was making reality bend wrong. His symbols glowed brighter, blue core-light pulsing hard.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Here's what I remember,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "You carry fragments, little one,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi stirred in my pouch. The faintest pulse of purple, then violet, then something I'd never seen—a color between colors, like Signal made visible. The device was sensing something in that suppression field.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Recognition.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Arc,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "The device..."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Another pause.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "I may be made from the very thing they believe will destroy Aethon."

emphasis_words: I may be made from the very thing they believe will destroy Aethon.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Quiet figure extended one hand. "Come with us, Arc. Let us examine what you carry. If your memories can be contained without waking—if we can understand the Builder's shutdown protocols—we might save the Pillars without needing to destroy what remains. The guilds would call it a fair deal. The Rootminders would call it wisdom. Even the Freeboters would call it survival."

emphasis_words: Come with us, Arc. Let us examine what you carry. If your memories can be contained without waking—if we can understand the Builder's shutdown protocols—we might save the Pillars without needing to destroy what remains. The guilds would call it a fair deal. The Rootminders would call it wisdom. Even the Freeboters would call it survival.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "No."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Then you are choosing destruction."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "I'm choosing to believe that understanding is better than silence."

emphasis_words: I'm choosing to believe that understanding is better than silence.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "The Forge Path also teaches that some metals are too brittle to forge,"

emphasis_words: The Forge Path also teaches that some metals are too brittle to forge,

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "It has saved more than you know."

emphasis_words: It has saved more than you know.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Yeah,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then two things happened at once.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi burst from my pouch in a brilliant flash of white light, its dome surface blazing as it shot straight at the device.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox, I believe I know how to shut it down. But you are not going to like the method."

emphasis_words: Miss Paradox, I believe I know how to shut it down. But you are not going to like the method.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi's white glow reflected off copper walls, turning the warehouse into a kaleidoscope of light and shadow. The device hovered three feet from the suppression field's center, its core window pulsing with colors I'd never seen in sequence before: purple-violet-white-silver-green, cycling faster than breath.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Quiet members moved, spreading to intercept. Not attacking—they'd meant it about not causing harm—but blocking, redirecting, trying to put themselves between Mochi and the device. They moved like Freeboters, I realized. Like people who worked outside the guilds, who'd learned to be efficient without backup.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Arc, whatever you're planning—"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "The suppression field operates by inverting Signal harmonics."

emphasis_words: The suppression field operates by inverting Signal harmonics.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: One of the Quiet members reached for Mochi. The device shot upward, bouncing off a ceiling beam, arcing back toward the suppression field. Playing keep-away with very serious people in a warehouse full of reality-warping technology. Just another day in the life.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive was moving—not toward the device, but toward the cable connections. His thick fingers worked at junction points, symbols glowing brighter as he traced power flows.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "These conduits,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "How right?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "I'll know when I see it."

emphasis_words: I'll know when I see it.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The lead Quiet member stopped trying to catch Mochi and turned to me. Even through the mask, I felt their gaze.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "You do not understand what you're interfering with. This technology is our only defense against what the Keepers will wake. We have spent decades studying Builder ruins, learning their engineering, their purposes. They built the Pillars to terraform this world, yes—but they also built shutdown protocols. Failsafes. This device is assembled from those failsafes. It is the Builders' own contingency plan."

emphasis_words: You do not understand what you're interfering with. This technology is our only defense against what the Keepers will wake. We have spent decades studying Builder ruins, learning their engineering, their purposes. They built the Pillars to terraform this world, yes—but they also built shutdown protocols. Failsafes. This device is assembled from those failsafes. It is the Builders' own contingency plan.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Then help me understand,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "We know that every Keeper awakening corresponds with Pillar instability. We know the Dimming accelerated after the first ancient memories were accessed. We know your companion"

emphasis_words: We know that every Keeper awakening corresponds with Pillar instability. We know the Dimming accelerated after the first ancient memories were accessed. We know your companion

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "You believe that,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Neither do you."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: They had a point. I hated that they had a point.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi dove at the device again, and this time made contact. The moment the glowing device touched the black surface, everything shifted.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The anti-rhythm stuttered.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: Arc's holographic form stabilized suddenly, perfectly clear. "I am detecting... Miss Paradox, Mochi is interfacing with the suppression field. Translating its patterns into—" He paused. "Into memory storage protocols."

emphasis_words: I am detecting... Miss Paradox, Mochi is interfacing with the suppression field. Translating its patterns into—

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "What?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "The device is not merely suppressing Signal. It is recording what it suppresses. Storing it. Isolating it from the network but preserving the data."

emphasis_words: The device is not merely suppressing Signal. It is recording what it suppresses. Storing it. Isolating it from the network but preserving the data.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Quiet member didn't deny it.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Because we are not monsters,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive's laugh was bitter. "Yeah. I've heard that song too. Played in every region, different words, same ending. It ends with silence and nobody remembering why."

emphasis_words: Yeah. I've heard that song too. Played in every region, different words, same ending. It ends with silence and nobody remembering why.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: His hands found the right junction. I saw it in the way his symbols flared, the way his blue core-light pulsed in triumph and grief together.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "This one,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "But what?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "You'll have maybe thirty seconds to grab it before they do. After that, fog clears, Harbor Guild comes running with the Marshals, this whole place becomes a crime scene. You take it, you're stealing—breaking the Covenant of Contract. Guild law, enforced by people who take oaths seriously. You leave it, they recover it."

emphasis_words: You'll have maybe thirty seconds to grab it before they do. After that, fog clears, Harbor Guild comes running with the Marshals, this whole place becomes a crime scene. You take it, you're stealing—breaking the Covenant of Contract. Guild law, enforced by people who take oaths seriously. You leave it, they recover it.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I looked at Arc. "Can you analyze it if we take it?"

emphasis_words: Can you analyze it if we take it?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Given time... yes. Though I confess the prospect of examining technology that mirrors my own construction is profoundly unsettling."

emphasis_words: Given time... yes. Though I confess the prospect of examining technology that mirrors my own construction is profoundly unsettling.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Will it help us understand what's happening?"

emphasis_words: Will it help us understand what's happening?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I looked at Mochi, still pressed against the device, glowing every color in sequence. The device was downloading something. Absorbing information it couldn't express but might desperately need.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I looked at the Quiet members, standing ready but not attacking. They believed they were saving the world. They'd sworn oaths to do it, witnessed by the Signal itself.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Maybe they were.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Clive,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: His thick fingers pulled the connection.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The device screamed.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Not sound—sensation. Like the Signal itself was crying out as the suppression field collapsed inward, centuries of stolen resonance flooding back through copper conduits. The warehouse blazed with light, then plunged into darkness, then stabilized into normal grey afternoon filtering through dirty skylights.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I lunged for the device. It was smaller than it looked—maybe the size of a breadbox—and still warm. Heavy, but manageable.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The Quiet members moved, but I was already running. Clive at my heels, Mochi shooting ahead like a luminous arrow, Arc's hologram flickering back to normal processing.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "You have just doomed yourself, Chronicler! When the Ark opens and the poison spreads, remember that we tried to stop it! Remember that we offered you mercy, sworn on every faith this world holds sacred!"

emphasis_words: You have just doomed yourself, Chronicler! When the Ark opens and the poison spreads, remember that we tried to stop it! Remember that we offered you mercy, sworn on every faith this world holds sacred!

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Then we were out the door, into afternoon sun that suddenly felt too bright, across docks where workers were emerging from buildings looking confused and relieved as the fog burned away. The shift-bells started ringing again. Guild banners snapped in the wind. Someone shouted about the Hammered One's blessing returning.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Three ships drifted in the harbor, crews shouting to each other in bewilderment. Alive. Safe.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We didn't stop running until we reached the street that led away from the waterfront, the device tucked under my arm and my heart hammering against my ribs.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Later, in a rented room above a copper-smith's shop—Freeboter territory, no guild questions asked—we examined our prize.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The device sat on the table, dark and geometrically impossible. Arc projected scanning beams across its surface, holographic data cascading around us like falling snow.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "This is extraordinary,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Because you're made of similar pieces?"

emphasis_words: Because you're made of similar pieces?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Perhaps. Or because whatever created me also created this. Or because..."

emphasis_words: Perhaps. Or because whatever created me also created this. Or because...

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The hologram flickered.

segments.speaker: arc
segments.text: "Miss Paradox, I must ask. If examining this device reveals that I am indeed dangerous—that my very existence threatens the Pillars—what will you do?"

emphasis_words: Miss Paradox, I must ask. If examining this device reveals that I am indeed dangerous—that my very existence threatens the Pillars—what will you do?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I looked at him. At the wristband that had been my partner for three years. At the AI who'd chosen to stay with me when he could have left. At the friend who worried about my safety even when his own existence was in question. At the being who'd awakened into sentience and spent every moment since trying to understand what that meant.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Then we figure out how to make you safe without making you silent,"

emphasis_words: Then we figure out how to make you safe without making you silent,

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: His holographic face shifted into what might have been gratitude.

segments.speaker: PAGE_TURN
segments.text: [DING - page turn cue]

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi sat on the table near the device, its dome glowing soft pink now that we were clear of the suppression field. But occasionally, it pulsed purple. Still processing whatever it had absorbed. Still carrying memories it couldn't speak.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Clive had climbed onto the windowsill, his compact form silhouetted against the harbor view. Guild workers flooding back to the docks, Marshals standing down, the Covenant of Contract holding as it always did. His symbols glowed faint blue, contemplative.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "They meant it, you know,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Because they believe they're right?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Because they might be."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: I sat down hard on the bed, feeling the weight of the day settle into my bones. "So what do we do?"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Same thing we always do."

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi bounced once, emitting a soft harmonic chord.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: "Yeah,"

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Outside, the sun was setting over Copperport. The Signal had returned. The ships were safe. The guilds were satisfied, the Marshals had stood down, and honest work was resuming like the Hammered One himself had blessed it.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: And under my bed, wrapped in canvas, sat a device that might hold answers to questions I wasn't sure I wanted to ask.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: *chronicle entry 006*: The Quiet is real. Not rumors, not paranoid salvagers—an organized movement operating across all regions, from Cuprum's guilds to Verdanya's groves. They're actively suppressing the Pillars, capturing memories they believe are toxic to the Signal itself. They've studied every faith, every philosophy, looking for justification. And they might be trying to save Aethon from catastrophic awakening.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Or they might be accelerating the very Dimming they fear.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: We captured one of their devices. Arc believes it was built using principles similar to his own construction—assembled from Builder shutdown protocols, failsafes designed to contain exactly what we're trying to wake. If The Quiet is right about the Keepers being dangerous, then Arc—who may be a Keeper himself—is carrying the very poison they're trying to quarantine.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: Mochi absorbed something from the device. Knowledge, maybe. Memory. Patterns it can't express but recognized on contact. The device isn't saying. Can't say. But I've seen that purple glow before, and it always means something ancient just woke up.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: The question isn't whether The Quiet believes they're right. It's whether they might actually be right.

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: And if they are... what does that make us?

segments.speaker: pixel
segments.text: -P.P.
